stubs.description: Parse XPM image data: header (width, height, num_colors, sym_size), sorted color table from color entries with key types (c/g/g4/m/s), then map pixel symbols to colors. Supports 1-4 channel output.
stubs.resolution: Full XPM parser implemented with symbol packing, binary-search color table, auto-detect channel count. Depends on new Color::try_parse and x11_colors.rs (676-entry X11 named color table).

stubs.description: Area-sampling when w >= 1.0 or h >= 1.0 (iterating over all covered pixels with fractional weights). Currently delegates to bilinear only, ignoring w/h params entirely (_w, _h).
stubs.resolution: Area-sampling implemented with i32 arithmetic, 0x8000 rounding bias, 65536/h scale, >>16 final shift. Handles all channel counts (1-4) in source. Falls back to bilinear when footprint is exactly 1x1.

stubs.description: Should work with all channel counts (1-4), converting color appropriately (grey for 1-ch, grey+alpha for 2-ch, etc.). Currently panics if channel_count != 4.
stubs.resolution: Match on channel_count (1-4) with appropriate color decomposition. 1-ch: to_grey(), 2-ch: [to_grey(), a()], 3-ch: [r,g,b], 4-ch: [r,g,b,a].

stubs.description: Same as fill — should work with all channel counts. Currently panics if channel_count != 4.
stubs.resolution: Same channel_count match pattern as fill, with rect clipping.

stubs.description: Should work with all channel counts, decomposing background color by channel and calling per-channel min/max rect. Returns None immediately if channel_count != 4.
stubs.resolution: Decompose bg color per channel_count into appropriate byte slice, then compare pixel slices.

stubs.description: Should handle cross-channel-count copies (all 16 src/dst combinations) with color conversion. Currently asserts channel counts must match.
stubs.resolution: Fast path when cc matches (copy_from_slice). Cross-channel path uses convert_pixel() helper implementing all 16 src×dst combinations with C++ grey formula ((r+g+b+1)/3).

stubs.description: Should work with all channel count combinations (src/dst can differ). Currently requires both to be 4-channel.
stubs.resolution: Removed both 4-channel assertions. Interpolated path computes sw/sh from inverse matrix and calls get_pixel_interpolated. Non-interpolated reads per src-cc, writes per dst-cc. get_transformed simplified (no longer needs 4-ch workaround).

stubs.description: Missing limit parameter — C++ aborts returning empty if unique colors exceed limit. Also C++ handles all channel counts while Rust requires 4-channel.
stubs.resolution: Added limit: usize parameter. Returns empty vec if set.len() > limit. Handles all channel counts by packing per-cc bytes into u32 key.

stubs.description: Missing optional channelCount parameter for converting channels during crop. Currently always preserves channel count.
stubs.resolution: Added out_cc: Option<u8> parameter. None preserves channel count. Some(cc) crops then converts via get_converted.

stubs.description: Missing optional channelCount parameter, same as get_cropped.
stubs.resolution: Added out_cc: Option<u8> parameter, passed through to get_cropped.

stubs.description: In C++ returns whether image wraps user-provided raw pointer. Rust set_user_map copies data into owned Vec, so always returns false correctly.
stubs.evidence: Rust deliberately copies user data (line 733). Returning false is correct behavior for API parity.

capabilities.summary: 11 stubs found, 10 Category A closed in 07f7760, 1 Category C not applicable. All multi-channel support gaps closed. Area-sampling, XPM parser, cross-channel conversion all implemented. Also added Color::try_parse (hex + X11 named colors) and x11_colors.rs as prerequisites. Fixed to_grey rounding to match C++ ((r+g+b+1)/3). Fixed get_converted grey formulas to match.

stubs.description: Should scroll item into view using View.Visit(). Only handles scroll-up; scroll-down case is unimplemented with dead let _ = item_bottom.
stubs.resolution: Added visible_height field (set during paint). scroll_to_index now scrolls down when item_bottom > scroll_y + visible_height.

stubs.description: Missing ReadOnly/disabled color logic. Hardcodes input_bg/fg/hl colors; should use output colors for ReadOnly mode and blend at 80% when disabled.
stubs.resolution: Added selection_mode and enabled fields to DefaultItemPanelBehavior. Selects output_* colors for ReadOnly, blends 80% toward bg_color when disabled.

stubs.description: Same missing ReadOnly/disabled color logic as DefaultItemPanelBehavior::paint, in the inline painting path.
stubs.resolution: Computes (bg, fg, hl) at top of paint based on selection_mode and enabled state, then uses throughout.

stubs.description: C++ Notice checks NF_FOCUS_CHANGED and clears KeyWalkChars when panel leaves focused path. No equivalent in Rust, so keywalk state persists after focus loss.
stubs.resolution: Added on_focus_changed() and on_enable_changed() methods to ListBox. ListBoxPanel in field_panel.rs implements notice() to propagate FOCUS_CHANGED and ENABLE_CHANGED.

stubs.description: Missing IsEnabled() check. C++ returns early if !IsEnabled(). Rust processes all input regardless of enabled state.
stubs.resolution: Added if !self.enabled { return false; } at top of input().

stubs.description: When keywalk finds no match, C++ calls screen->Beep(). Rust silently clears keywalk_chars.
stubs.evidence: No Screen or Beep abstraction in the Rust codebase. Depends on platform Screen API not yet ported.

stubs.description: Does not call base expand/shrink, does not validate ItemPanelInterface (C++ fatal errors if missing), does not call base RasterGroup methods.
stubs.resolution: Architectural difference makes this irrelevant. C++ ListBox inherits emRasterGroup and must call base AutoExpand/AutoShrink; Rust composes a RasterLayout as a child panel in the tree (created in create_item_children). C++ emFatalError for missing interface catches virtual-dispatch programmer errors; Rust create_item_panel always succeeds (factory or DefaultItemPanel fallback, no virtual dispatch). No base class calls needed.

stubs.description: Original diagnosis said C++ uses subsequence matching. Re-analysis shows C++ uses naive substring search (reset needle index on mismatch, advance haystack by 1) — same semantics as str::contains().
stubs.resolution: No change needed. Rust contains() already matches C++ behavior. Original diagnosis was incorrect about subsequence vs substring.

capabilities.summary: 8 stubs found. 5 Category A closed in 4e8eae0, 1 verified already correct, 1 reclassified as not_applicable (auto_expand — Rust uses composition not inheritance, no base class to call, create_item_panel always succeeds), 1 Category D blocked (beep, needs Screen API).

stubs.description: Empty virtual hook for subclass override. C++ base implementation is also empty. Rust uses callback pattern (on_selection, on_selection_signal) instead.
stubs.evidence: C++ base is void emTextField::SelectionChanged() {}. Rust callbacks at lines 354-364 replace virtual dispatch.

stubs.description: Cursor drawn as simple rectangle instead of C++ I-beam polygon (8-vertex with serifs for insert) or frame polygon (10-vertex with inner cutout for overwrite).
stubs.resolution: Overwrite cursor: 10-vertex frame polygon with d=ch*0.07. Insert cursor: 8-vertex I-beam with serifs (d1=d*0.5, d2=d*2.2). Both use paint_polygon. Applied to both single-line and multi-line paint paths.

stubs.description: Multi-line selection drawn as per-row rectangles instead of single 8-vertex polygon spanning row boundaries.
stubs.resolution: Single-row: paint_rect. Multi-row: 8-vertex polygon using index_to_col_row for start/end positions, spanning from start column through right edge to end column.

stubs.description: Missing cursor transparency: 75% for read-only, 88% for blink-off. Rust does not render cursor at all when not focused but has no transparency levels.
stubs.resolution: GetTransparented(75) = 25% opaque: with_alpha(a * 0.25). GetTransparented(88) = 12% opaque: with_alpha(a * 0.12). Applied to both single-line and multi-line cursor paths.

stubs.description: Read-only fields should use OutputBg/Fg/Hl colors instead of InputBg/Fg/Hl. Rust always uses input colors with lerp dimming for disabled.
stubs.resolution: Non-editable fields now use look.output_fg_color instead of dimmed input_fg_color. Applied to both single-line and multi-line paint paths.

capabilities.summary: 5 stubs found. 4 Category A closed in 121533c, 1 Category C not applicable. All visual fidelity gaps addressed: cursor shape (polygon), selection polygon, cursor transparency, output field colors.

stubs.description: Should move mouse pointer by (dx,dy) relative to current position. C++ accumulates deltas and applies via XWarpPointer. Disabled on XWayland.
stubs.evidence: No-op with debug log. Winit has no relative mouse warp API. Would need platform-specific code (XWarpPointer, CGWarpMouseCursorPosition, SetCursorPos).

stubs.description: Should emit acoustic warning beep. C++ calls XBell on X11.
stubs.evidence: No-op with debug log. Winit does not expose beep/bell API. Needs platform calls (XBell, NSBeep, MessageBeep).

stubs.description: Counter tracking is correct. Missing actual screensaver inhibition: C++ uses 59-second timer with XResetScreenSaver + xscreensaver-command -deactivate.
stubs.evidence: Counter-only with debug log. Needs D-Bus org.freedesktop.ScreenSaver.Inhibit or X11 reset approach.

stubs.description: Should return dedicated signal firing when window flags change. Returns close_signal as placeholder. set_window_flags sets flags_changed=true as workaround but cannot fire a signal (no &mut Scheduler reference).
stubs.evidence: Comment and TODO say 'allocate a dedicated signal when the scheduler supports it.' Design/plumbing issue.

stubs.description: Should return actual window decoration border sizes from _NET_FRAME_EXTENTS. Returns hardcoded defaults (4,30,4,4 decorated; 0,0,0,0 undecorated).
stubs.evidence: Comment says 'Winit does not expose decoration sizes directly.' Hardcoded values will be wrong on many WMs.

capabilities.summary: 5 stubs found. 4 are Category B (platform limitation — winit lacks mouse warp, beep, screensaver, and decoration size APIs). 1 is Category D (window_flags_signal blocked on scheduler signal plumbing). No Category A stubs — all real gaps require platform integration beyond current deps. No changes in Phase 6b.

Zero stubs found. All 16 methods fully implemented with correct fixed-point arithmetic (i64 intermediate for multiply, bitwise floor/ceil/round). Confirmed covered by 42 painter golden tests. Reclassify from stub to verified.

commits: 07f7760 fix(CAP-0033): close 10 Image stubs — multi-channel support, XPM parser, area sampling
4e8eae0 fix(CAP-0041): close ListBox stubs — disabled state, scroll-down, focus cleanup
121533c fix(CAP-0069): close TextField stubs — cursor shape, selection polygon, output colors
prerequisites_added: Color::try_parse — hex format extensions + X11 named color lookup
x11_colors.rs — 676-entry sorted X11 named color table
to_grey rounding fix — (r+g+b+1)/3 matching C++ GetGrey
convert_pixel() helper — all 16 channel conversion combinations
ListBoxPanel: notice() handler for FOCUS_CHANGED, ENABLE_CHANGED
phase_6b_summary.test_results: 1096 tests pass (611 unit + 216 integration + 191 golden + 78 other). Zero golden test regressions.
